shots.production_prompt: Kanye West Charlie Rose interview 2005 NBA discussion — full exchange where Kanye claims he could make the NBA today, Charlie Rose responds skeptically 'no you wouldn't.' Need close-ups of both faces showing the back-and-forth.
alternates: Charlie Rose Kanye West interview 2005 clip: NBA basketball discussion segment
YouTube: Kanye West tells Charlie Rose he could play in NBA 2005 interview

shots.production_prompt: Portrait of philosopher William James, 1890s Harvard professor era: bearded, intense gaze, seated in academic study with bookshelves background, sepia-toned photographic style. Overlay text in elegant serif typeface: 'The Will to Believe' (1896). Then transition to clean text card on off-white background showing James's quote in dark charcoal Garamond font, centered, generous margins, academic journal aesthetic.
alternates: Split composition: Left side shows William James circa 1890 in Victorian academic attire, right side displays 'The Will to Believe (1896)' in classic book title typography with ornate border
William James philosopher portrait with dense library backdrop, warm amber lighting, followed by minimalist text slide: title and quote in clean sans-serif on cream background

shots.production_prompt: Jean-Paul Sartre portrait 1960s Paris: philosopher seated at café table, cigarette in hand, black turtleneck, existentialist intellectual atmosphere, moody black-and-white photography style with slight film grain. Overlay text: 'Bad Faith (mauvaise foi)' in bold modernist typography. Then cut to definition card: 'Acting as if you're not free to choose' — clean Helvetica on matte black background, white text, simple underline accent.
alternates: Sartre in Left Bank café setting, smoke-filled atmosphere, eyeglasses reflecting light, paired with bilingual text treatment: 'Mauvaise Foi / Bad Faith' and definition in French New Wave film title style
Close-up of Sartre's contemplative expression, Saint-Germain-des-Prés café backdrop, followed by minimalist infographic: term definition with visual icon of crossed-out chains

shots.production_prompt: Bold typographic sequence on neutral gradient background (cream to light gray): First frame shows 'EXISTENCE PRECEDES ESSENCE' in strong geometric sans-serif, all caps, centered. Smooth dissolve transition to second frame: 'Your life is what you make it.' in slightly softer weight, sentence case, same typeface family. Subtle texture overlay — paper grain or canvas weave — adds warmth. Each phrase holds for 2-3 seconds. Minimalist, confident, manifesto aesthetic.
alternates: Animated text reveal: 'Existence Precedes Essence' fades in over abstract brushstroke texture, then morphs into 'Your Life Is What You Make It' — modern editorial design, high contrast black text on white
Split-screen layout: philosophical term on left in academic serif, plain-language translation on right in bold sans-serif, connected by subtle arrow or equals sign, clean infographic style
